etymology.description: 冎 (bone) + 刂 (knife) = to cut up bone and flesh -> stylized as 別
etymology.interpretation: The character 別 (bié, "separate") originally depicted the action of cutting apart bones, highlighting the idea of separation. It combines the components 冎 (guǎ, "bone") and 刂 (dāo, "knife") to convey this meaning.
etymology.simplification: The simplified form 别 is a variation of 別 where the component 冎 (guǎ, "bone") is replaced by the simpler 另 (lìng, "other"), maintaining the concept of separation.

examples.chinese: 我们在车站告别(我們在車站告別)。
examples.english: We said farewell at the station.

examples.chinese: 她别着一朵花在头上(她別著一朵花在頭上)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bié zhe yī duǒ huā zài tóushàng.
examples.english: She pinned a flower on her head.

examples.chinese: 请别打扰我(請別打擾我)。
